title: Terminal hard-sigmoid direct output
hypothesis: With accepted lower-layer hard-sigmoid grounding in place, bounded terminal HardSigmoid vec-op tails on fixed nm1 should benefit from writing directly to Y_DRAM instead of staging through activation SRAM.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Does bounded terminal hard-sigmoid direct vec-op output improve latency and energy on fixed nm1 against the non-fused baseline?
include: one bounded hard-sigmoid measurement baseline on fixed nm1
one paired direct-output candidate on the same model suite
exclude: broad nonlinear-family ranking beyond hard-sigmoid in the first pass
full npu_top physical claims beyond the accepted reduced hard-sigmoid proxy grounding
follow_on_broad_sweep: optional wider nonlinear-family sweep only after the bounded hard-sigmoid comparison is accepted

expected_benefit: reduced terminal writeback latency for hard-sigmoid vec-op tails
lower energy from removing a redundant SRAM write/read pair
risks: hard-sigmoid perf gains may be smaller than sigmoid on the chosen bounded suite
reduced proxy physical grounding is sufficient for this staged proof but not for broad full-top ranking

expected_result.reason: first collect non-fused legality and reference metrics on the bounded hard-sigmoid suite
direct vec-op output should remove the final activation-SRAM writeback roundtrip on the bounded hard-sigmoid suite
